prompt: Construct a ForgeOps Fulfillment & Planning Console supporting offline-first operations for a fabrication-and-delivery team that manages customer collaboration requests, build plans (BOM-based), deliveries, internal settlement tracking, notifications, and long-running computations in a single Svelte Single Page Application. Users include Administrator (configures rules and permissions), Sales Coordinator (captures collaboration intent/leads), Planner (creates and versions build plans), Dispatcher (schedules deliveries and captures proof-of-delivery), and Auditor (read-only review of logs and ledgers). The Svelte UI provides responsive routing between Lead Inbox, Plan Workspace, Delivery Calendar, Ledger, and Notification Center, using Table views for lists, a Drawer for record detail, and Modal dialogs for edits and confirmations. Lead intake uses standardized forms (requirements, budget, availability window, contact) with required-field validation; new leads auto-assign by round-robin to active Sales Coordinators and move through New → In Discussion → Quoted → Confirmed → Closed, with SLA reminders at 24 hours without an update and "key node" notifications on status transitions. Plan management supports create/copy/compare/rollback with per-change BOM diffs, tags, and notes; users can generate a share link token that grants view-only access locally (e.g., "valid for 7 days" and revocable). Fulfillment covers delivery coverage rules (ZIP ranges, maximum 120 miles from a depot), freight rules (base $45 + $1.25/mile after 20 miles; oversize surcharge $75 when any item length > 8 ft), scheduling time windows (8:00 AM–6:00 PM in 30-minute slots), delivery order generation, proof-of-delivery receipts (signature name + timestamp + optional photo attachment), and exception records for reschedule/refused/loss-damage. The Notification Center is strictly in-app, with templates, variables, subscriptions, Do Not Disturb (e.g., 9:00 PM–7:00 AM), delivery/read receipts, and a visible retry queue for failed local dispatch. Settlement and fund security are handled as an internal escrow-style ledger (freeze/unfreeze, milestone or one-time settlement, refunds/partial refunds, withdrawals), producing printable invoices/vouchers without initiating real payments. All functionality runs fully offline in the browser with no independent backend: business logic is implemented in a frontend service layer processing local mock data and user inputs, and persistence uses IndexedDB as the primary "database" plus LocalStorage for session flags and UI preferences. Import/export and backups use browser-side file handling (Blob download/upload) with encrypted backup bundles (AES-256 using a user-provided passphrase) and recovery that validates file format and fingerprint (SHA-256) before restoring. Pseudo-login uses locally stored usernames with salted password hashes (Argon2 or PBKDF2), session idle timeout at 15 minutes, and role-based access control enforced in routing guards and service calls; permission changes are audited with immutable local entries. Security includes strict input validation, output encoding to prevent injection/XSS, masked display of sensitive fields (show last 4 digits of bank account references), and an access/operation log retained for 180 days with anomaly alerts when more than 10 failed logins occur in 5 minutes. "Pluggable third-party delivery APIs" are implemented as an adapter interface with offline stubs and a local queue that can be exported as a file for later integration testing, without any network calls. Async jobs run in Web Workers with a local compute queue for large BOM compares, bulk delivery generation, and ledger reconciliation; jobs expose progress %, support pause/resume, cache intermediate results, and trigger in-app alerts when runtime exceeds 30 seconds or when error rates exceed 2% in a rolling window of 50 jobs.
